title: jatropha horizontalis
intro: Jatropha horizontalis, also known as the cat's claw or cat's claw vine, is a flowering plant native to Central and South America. It is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, and its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Jatropha horizontalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms associated with arthritis, fever, and other infections. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves or stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, and are often used to support overall well-being and promote recovery from illness.
